nodes.label: Task Implementation Loop (Apply)
nodes.rationale: Keeps going through tasks until done or blocked; keeps code changes minimal and scoped; pauses on ambiguity, errors, or unclear requirements rather than guessing.

nodes.label: Delta Spec Sync Assessment
nodes.rationale: Compares delta specs against main specs before archiving to determine what changes would be applied (adds, modifications, removals, renames). User chooses sync-now or skip.

nodes.label: Explore Mode Stance (Think, Do Not Implement)
nodes.rationale: A stance, not a workflow. No fixed steps, no mandatory outputs. Curious, visual (ASCII diagrams), adaptive, patient. May create OpenSpec artifacts but never write application code.
nodes.norm_label: explore mode stance (think, do not implement)

nodes.label: Artifact Generation Flow (Propose)
nodes.rationale: Creates artifacts in dependency order until apply-ready: proposal.md, design.md, tasks.md. Uses schema's apply.requires to determine which artifacts are needed before implementation.

nodes.label: Graphify Full Pipeline (Detect → Extract → Build → Cluster → Analyze → Report → Viz)
nodes.rationale: Drop any folder of files into graphify and get a queryable knowledge graph. Persistent across sessions, honest audit trail (EXTRACTED/INFERRED/AMBIGUOUS), community detection surfaces cross-document connections.
nodes.norm_label: graphify full pipeline (detect → extract → build → cluster → analyze → report → viz)

nodes.label: EXTRACTED/INFERRED/AMBIGUOUS Confidence System
nodes.rationale: Every edge must be tagged with confidence. EXTRACTED=explicit in source, INFERRED=reasonable inference from structure/domain, AMBIGUOUS=uncertain. Honesty rules: never invent an edge.

nodes.label: Graphify Honesty Rules
nodes.rationale: Never invent an edge. Never skip corpus check warning. Always show token cost. Never hide cohesion scores. Never run HTML viz on >5,000 nodes without warning.

nodes.label: Graphify Node ID Format Rules
nodes.rationale: IDs must be deterministic: {parent_dir}_{filename_without_ext}_{entity}, lowercase [a-z0-9_]. AST extractor generates matching IDs; mismatch creates ghost-duplicate nodes.

nodes.label: Confidence Score Rubric (0.55-0.95 discrete)
nodes.rationale: EXTRACTED always 1.0. INFERRED picks from discrete set: 0.95 (structural evidence), 0.85 (strong functional alignment), 0.75 (shared problem domain), 0.65 (weak thematic), 0.55 (speculative). Never use 0.5 as default.

nodes.label: Code-Only Update Optimization (Skip LLM)
nodes.rationale: When --update detects only code file changes, semantic extraction is skipped entirely. Only AST extraction runs. Saves tokens and time since code-only changes don't need LLM re-extraction.

nodes.label: Graphify Watch Mode (Auto-Rebuild on File Changes)
nodes.norm_label: graphify watch mode (auto-rebuild on file changes)

nodes.label: Graphify Extra Exports (Wiki, Neo4j, SVG, GraphML, MCP)
nodes.norm_label: graphify extra exports (wiki, neo4j, svg, graphml, mcp)

nodes.label: Graphify GitHub Clone and Cross-Repo Merge
nodes.norm_label: graphify github clone and cross-repo merge

nodes.label: Query-First Rule for Codebase Questions
nodes.rationale: For codebase questions, first run graphify query when graph exists. Use path/explain for relationships and focused concepts. These return scoped subgraphs smaller than GRAPH_REPORT.md or raw grep.

nodes.label: Update Graph After Code Changes
nodes.rationale: After modifying code, run 'graphify update .' to keep the graph current (AST-only, no API cost).

nodes.label: Custom Tailwind Color Palette (Principal, TextoBeige, FondoApp, FondoCentral)
nodes.norm_label: custom tailwind color palette (principal, textobeige, fondoapp, fondocentral)

nodes.label: Client-Server WebSocket Architecture (Phase 1)
nodes.rationale: Uses native WebSockets over SignalR for lower latency; Python chosen as MediaPipe's native environment for Heavy model. Client (Blazor WASM) captures camera, server (Python) runs inference.

nodes.label: Mirror Effect (X Coordinate Inversion)
nodes.rationale: X_espejo = 1.0 - X_original. MediaPipe's left shoulder (landmark 11) maps to avatar's right arm on screen, creating natural mirror behavior.

nodes.label: Torso 3D Rotation Simulation (Depth Attenuation + Clamping)
nodes.rationale: Uses MediaPipe Z coordinate difference between shoulders, attenuated by sensitivity factor and clamped to max ±25° Y rotation. CSS: perspective(600px) rotateY(anguloY) rotateZ(anguloX). Prevents avatar from turning its back.

nodes.label: Arm Kinematics (Atan2 Angle Calculation)
nodes.rationale: Uses Math.Atan2 on MediaPipe coordinates to compute shoulder-to-elbow and elbow-to-wrist angles. Arm segments have fixed pixel lengths; only rotation changes. Local elbow angle = global_angle - shoulder_angle.

nodes.label: Rest Position and Arm Rigidity Rule
nodes.rationale: Arms have fixed pixel size, NEVER scale. When wrist visibility falls below threshold, arms gradually return to 0° (hanging vertically against torso). Coordinates only drive rotation, never stretch.
nodes.norm_label: rest position and arm rigidity rule

nodes.label: Arm Rigidity CSS Rules (Fixed Size, Transform-Origin Anchoring)
nodes.rationale: Arms use position:absolute and transform-origin:top center for rigid permanent anchoring to shoulder joints. Fixed size prevents deformation from camera errors. Neutral state = rotate(0deg), arms hanging vertically.
nodes.norm_label: arm rigidity css rules (fixed size, transform-origin anchoring)

nodes.label: CSS 3D Perspective (800px) for Torso
nodes.rationale: Center panel has perspective:800px to enable depth space for torso rotation. Body-wrapper applies rotateY based on computed shoulder depth difference, giving immersion without a 3D engine.

nodes.label: Avatar CSS System (Body Wrapper, Torso, Head, Arms, Items)
nodes.norm_label: avatar css system (body wrapper, torso, head, arms, items)

nodes.label: Body Wrapper with preserve-3d and rotateY
nodes.norm_label: body wrapper with preserve-3d and rotatey

nodes.label: Customization UI (Shirts, Hats, Glasses, Hair, Hand Items)
nodes.norm_label: customization ui (shirts, hats, glasses, hair, hand items)

nodes.label: Simulation Test Controls (Torso, Head, Arm Ranges)
nodes.norm_label: simulation test controls (torso, head, arm ranges)

nodes.label: Build Avatar Tracking System Change (Archived 2026-06-04, spec-driven)
nodes.norm_label: build avatar tracking system change (archived 2026-06-04, spec-driven)

nodes.label: Native WebSocket over SignalR Decision
nodes.rationale: SignalR añade overhead de negociación HTTP, fallback a long-polling, y serialización JSON automática que no es necesaria. WebSocket nativo da control total sobre el ciclo de vida de la conexión, formato de payload binario/texto, y minimiza latencia. El doc de arquitectura explícitamente recomienda evitar SignalR.

nodes.label: JPEG Binary Upload + JSON Minified Response Format
nodes.rationale: Enviar JPEG binario en lugar de Base64 reduce el payload ~33%. La respuesta JSON es mínima: solo 6 puntos de pose + expresión facial + visibilidad. Se eligió que el servidor devuelva coordenadas normalizadas (0-1) en lugar de ángulos precalculados para mantener el servidor stateless y permitir que el cliente aplique calibración y espejo.
nodes.norm_label: jpeg binary upload + json minified response format

nodes.label: Client-Side Transform Pipeline (Mirror + Angles + Lerp)
nodes.rationale: Toda la lógica de transformación (espejo, ángulos, calibración, Lerp) corre en C# en Blazor. El servidor solo hace inferencia de MediaPipe. Pipeline: Landmarks crudos → Calibración (restar offset) → Espejo (X = 1.0 - X) → Ángulos (Atan2) → Lerp → CSS transform. Mantiene el servidor Python simple y stateless.

nodes.label: 3-Column Grid Blazor Component Layout
nodes.rationale: Layout grid 3 columnas (20%-60%-20%) con componentes Razor independientes: CameraPanel (izq, 20%), AvatarRenderer con Torso/Head/Arm (centro, 60%), CustomizationPanel (der, 20%). Servicios: AvatarState (Singleton), TrackingService (Scoped), CameraService (JS interop).

nodes.label: CSS 3D Torso Rotation with Clamping
nodes.rationale: Contenedor .body-wrapper recibe transform: rotateY(angulo) calculado a partir de diferencia de profundidad (Z) entre hombros. Ángulo limitado a ±30° con factor de sensibilidad bajo (~0.5). Contenedor central con perspective: 800px. Sin clamping, diferencias grandes producirían rotaciones no naturales.

nodes.label: Lerp Interpolation Smoothing (t=0.3)
nodes.rationale: Interpolación lineal entre valor actual y objetivo con factor t=0.3 (30% hacia el target por frame). Buen balance entre respuesta rápida y eliminación de jitter. Valores menores (0.1) se sienten pesados, valores mayores (0.8) no filtran suficiente. Se aplica a todos los ángulos: hombros, codos, torso y cabeza.

nodes.label: Client-Side Calibration Offset System
nodes.rationale: Botón Calibrar captura 2 segundos de landmarks (60 frames a 30 FPS), calcula promedios, y los guarda como CalibrationOffset en el cliente. Los offsets se restan de cada landmark entrante antes de calcular ángulos. Hacer la calibración en el cliente mantiene el servidor stateless. Cada usuario tiene su propio offset.

nodes.label: Heartbeat + Exponential Backoff Reconnection Protocol
nodes.rationale: Ping/pong cada 5s con timeout de 3s. Backoff exponencial: 1s → 2s → 4s → 8s → 16s max. Tras 5 intentos fallidos mostrar toast 'Conexión perdida'. Throttling adaptativo: RTT >100ms → 20 FPS, >200ms → 15 FPS, <50ms → 30 FPS. Crítico en WiFi de expo congestionada.

nodes.label: WSS/HTTPS Security for Production
nodes.rationale: En desarrollo usar ws://localhost:8765 y http://localhost:5000. En producción/expo configurar certificado autofirmado y usar wss:// y https://. getUserMedia solo funciona en contextos seguros. Para demo en red local, certificado autofirmado es suficiente.

nodes.label: Fallback Demo Mode Without Camera
nodes.rationale: Si getUserMedia falla o el usuario rechaza permisos, el sistema entra en modo demo: avatar en posición neutra, sliders manuales del panel izquierdo permiten simular movimiento. Panel de personalización sigue 100% funcional. No se debe mostrar una pantalla rota o vacía.

nodes.label: AvatarState Singleton with OnChange Event Pattern
nodes.rationale: AvatarState.cs registrado como Singleton en DI de Blazor WASM. Expone propiedades (ShirtStyle, HatStyle, GlassesStyle, etc.) y evento OnChange. Los componentes se suscriben y re-renderizan al cambiar el estado. Singleton asegura estado compartido; patrón event-based evita acoplar componentes entre sí.
nodes.norm_label: avatarstate singleton with onchange event pattern

nodes.label: Tailwind CDN + Native CSS Strategy
nodes.rationale: Tailwind CDN en index.html para layout, utilidades y colores de la paleta (principal: #990066, textoBeige, fondoApp: #1a1a1a, fondoCentral: #2a2a35). CSS nativo en archivos .css por componente para transformaciones 3D y reglas complejas (sombras, keyframes, pseudo-elementos).

nodes.label: Interactive AI Avatar System for Tech Expo
nodes.norm_label: interactive ai avatar system for tech expo

nodes.label: Explicit [Parameter] Data Flow Instead of @inject AvatorState
nodes.rationale: Blazor solo re-renderiza componentes hijos cuando sus [Parameter] cambian (optimización ShouldRender). Al leer AvatarState vía @inject, los cambios de personalización nunca disparan re-render porque los parámetros cinemáticos no cambian. Solución: AvatarRenderer lee TODO de AvatarState y pasa valores como [Parameter] a hijos, estableciendo flujo unidireccional canónico de Blazor.
nodes.norm_label: explicit [parameter] data flow instead of @inject avatorstate

nodes.label: Avatar Scale 2× via CSS transform
nodes.rationale: Agregar scale(2) al final de la cadena de transform existente en TorsoTransform con transform-origin: center bottom. El avatar ocupa solo ~15% del panel central (180px en panel de ~1152px). Escalar 2× lo lleva a 360×500px visuales, ocupación adecuada. Alternativa de reescribir todos los px en CSS (~30 valores) es frágil.

nodes.label: Arm Upper Segment Color via CSS Sibling Selectors
nodes.rationale: Eliminar background: inherit del markup de arm-upper. Usar CSS con selector de hermano general ~: .arm-upper { background-color: var(--skin-color) } por defecto; .torso.shirt-long ~ .arm-container .arm-upper { background-color: #2b2d42 } para manga larga. Evita dependencia frágil de herencia.
nodes.norm_label: arm upper segment color via css sibling selectors

nodes.label: Modified Avatar Customization - Parameter Flow Guarantee
nodes.norm_label: modified avatar customization - parameter flow guarantee

nodes.label: Modified Avatar Rendering - Explicit Parameters for Customization
nodes.norm_label: modified avatar rendering - explicit parameters for customization

nodes.label: Unidirectional Data Flow Requirement (Blazor Canonical Pattern)
nodes.norm_label: unidirectional data flow requirement (blazor canonical pattern)

nodes.label: Server.py continue Indentation Bug Fix
nodes.rationale: Un continue mal indentado en server.py descarta todos los mensajes binarios — solo los mensajes de texto (ping) son procesados. Corregido el indent, el servidor procesa frames correctamente. Causa raíz del tracking no funcional a pesar de cámara conectada.

nodes.label: Real-Time Skeleton Overlay on Camera View
nodes.rationale: MediaPipe ya detecta los 33 landmarks de pose. Para feedback visual se necesita enviar todos los landmarks y dibujar líneas de conexión. Usar canvas overlay (no SVG/DOM) para dibujo eficiente sin crear/eliminar elementos DOM en cada frame. Conexiones del esqueleto son fijas (topología MediaPipe Pose).
nodes.norm_label: real-time skeleton overlay on camera view

nodes.label: Canvas Overlay Approach Instead of SVG/DOM
nodes.rationale: Un canvas permite dibujar líneas y puntos eficientemente sin crear/eliminar elementos DOM en cada frame. El canvas se posiciona con position: absolute sobre el <video> de la cámara. Dibujar 33 puntos + ~11 líneas por frame es operación trivial (<1ms).
nodes.norm_label: canvas overlay approach instead of svg/dom

nodes.label: Normalized Landmark Coordinates Protocol (0-1)
nodes.rationale: El servidor envía landmarks normalizados. El cliente convierte a píxeles usando las dimensiones del canvas. Esto mantiene el protocolo independiente de resolución. El campo skeleton incluye width/height del frame original para la conversión.

nodes.label: Modified WebSocket Tracking - Correct Binary Frame Processing
nodes.norm_label: modified websocket tracking - correct binary frame processing

nodes.label: CSS Transition Removal for Double Smoothing Fix

nodes.label: Accordion UI Pattern for Customization Categories

nodes.label: Responsive Avatar and Dynamic WS — Proposal
nodes.norm_label: responsive avatar and dynamic ws — proposal

nodes.label: Responsive Avatar and Dynamic WS — Tasks
nodes.norm_label: responsive avatar and dynamic ws — tasks

nodes.label: manualScale null-override pattern (null=auto, number=manual)
nodes.rationale: Simple boolean-free state: null means auto mode (dynamic calculation), any number means manual override. Avoids complex state machines.

nodes.label: Hamburger menu collapse pattern for scale slider UI
nodes.rationale: Slider menu hid behind hamburger toggle button using CSS max-height+overflow:hidden for animated collapse without JS. Does not occupy permanent space when unused.
nodes.norm_label: hamburger menu collapse pattern for scale slider ui

nodes.label: Direct JS interop for slider (C# to JS, bypassing Blazor state)
nodes.rationale: Slider calls JS.InvokeVoidAsync('avatarAnim.setManualScale') directly instead of routing through AvatarState C#→Blazor→JS. Avoids unnecessary cycle; ~0.1ms per call at 60fps is negligible.
nodes.norm_label: direct js interop for slider (c# to js, bypassing blazor state)

nodes.label: overflow:hidden on panel-central for avatar containment
nodes.rationale: Simplest way to clip avatar overflow when arms/head extend beyond panel bounds. Symmetric clipping since avatar is centered. Mitigated by 0.85 safety factor.
nodes.norm_label: overflow:hidden on panel-central for avatar containment

nodes.label: Dynamic avatar scale from container dimensions (Math.min(cw/180, ch/400)*0.85)
nodes.rationale: Replaces hardcoded scale(2) with container-responsive calculation. Uses body-wrapper base width 180px and estimated avatar height 400px. 0.85 safety factor prevents edge contact. Recalculated each update() frame for window resize adaptation.
nodes.norm_label: dynamic avatar scale from container dimensions (math.min(cw/180, ch/400)*0.85)

nodes.label: WebSocket URL derived from HostEnvironment.BaseAddress
nodes.rationale: Replaces hardcoded ws://localhost:8765 with dynamic derivation from HTTP host. Enables other devices on network to connect. Avoids JS interop since BaseAddress already contains the host. Risk: ws:// may fail with HTTPS (mixed content).

nodes.label: JS rotation compensation (baseRotation - shoulderAngle - elbowAngle)
nodes.rationale: CSS cannot read tracking angles (JS-only), so compensation runs in avatarAnim.update(). Subtracts accumulated shoulder+elbow angles to cancel inherited rotation, then applies ±90° base for outward orientation. Sign errors would cause double rotation instead of cancellation.

nodes.label: stay-upright CSS class as opt-in for upright hand items
nodes.rationale: Instead of Blazor boolean parameter, uses hardcoded CSS class. Items with stay-upright (fernet, water, cup) get compensation; items without (future watch, bracelet) follow arm rotation naturally. Simpler than adding per-item Blazor parameters.
nodes.norm_label: stay-upright css class as opt-in for upright hand items

nodes.label: querySelectorAll('.item.stay-upright.active') per-frame loop
nodes.rationale: DOM query each update() frame to find active upright items. ~0.1ms for 3 elements, negligible performance impact. Uses closest('.arm-container.left') to determine handedness for sign direction.

nodes.label: Avatar scale slider range 0.5-3 with step 0.1
nodes.norm_label: avatar scale slider range 0.5-3 with step 0.1

nodes.label: Auto button to restore dynamic scale calculation
nodes.norm_label: auto button to restore dynamic scale calculation

nodes.norm_label: proportional scaling of full body-wrapper (not individual parts)

nodes.label: body-wrapper base dimensions: width 180px, total avatar height ~400px
nodes.norm_label: body-wrapper base dimensions: width 180px, total avatar height ~400px

nodes.label: 0.85 safety factor for dynamic scale to prevent edge contact
nodes.norm_label: 0.85 safety factor for dynamic scale to prevent edge contact

nodes.label: Arm container handedness detection via closest('.arm-container.left')
nodes.norm_label: arm container handedness detection via closest('.arm-container.left')

nodes.norm_label: camerapanel scale menu with hamburger toggle

nodes.label: Avatar overflow fix via overflow:hidden + dynamic scale
nodes.norm_label: avatar overflow fix via overflow:hidden + dynamic scale

nodes.label: WebSocket URL from HTTP host instead of hardcoded localhost
